claim: AI in CI/CD must start with read-only co-pilot patterns: summarize diffs, suggest tests, explain failures—but humans always decide and approve changes.
actionable: Implement AI in pipelines first as read-only analysis tools; require explicit human review before any AI-suggested change is applied.
source_title: Keynote: AI in CI/CD Without the Hype: Practical Patterns for Platform Engineers

claim: Prompt injection is a critical vulnerability when AI agents read untrusted content (PR comments, commit messages) that can be written by attackers.
actionable: Never allow AI agents to execute actions based on content in PRs, commits, or logs without validation; treat AI output as untrusted.
source_title: Keynote: AI in CI/CD Without the Hype: Practical Patterns for Platform Engineers

claim: CI/CD pipelines are high-value targets; they read source, secrets, credentials and can push to production—compromising a pipeline is better than compromising an endpoint.
actionable: Treat AI agents in pipelines with the same security rigor as human accounts; enforce least privilege, audit logging, and access controls.
source_title: Keynote: AI in CI/CD Without the Hype: Practical Patterns for Platform Engineers

claim: The Axios npm compromise (March 2026) exploited a single compromised maintainer account to inject a backdoor via post-install hooks—exemplifying supply chain risk.
actionable: Implement dependency scanning and AI-assisted anomaly detection for package updates; monitor unexpected post-install hooks.
source_title: Keynote: AI in CI/CD Without the Hype: Practical Patterns for Platform Engineers

claim: Use dual-AI review pattern: first AI generates, second AI (as rubber ducky) questions and finds gaps, then human makes final decision.
actionable: Chain two AI models in a skeptical review loop before human approval to catch errors the primary model misses.
source_title: Keynote: AI in CI/CD Without the Hype: Practical Patterns for Platform Engineers

claim: AI agents reading logs and stack traces to identify root causes is high-value; humans review the diagnosis and decide whether to apply the fix.
actionable: Use AI for log analysis and root cause identification in on-call workflows; engineer reviews suggestion before executing remediation.
source_title: Keynote: AI in CI/CD Without the Hype: Practical Patterns for Platform Engineers

claim: AI-assisted alert deduplication reduces false positives and alert fatigue, but humans must still decide what to act on; AI should not close tickets.
actionable: Use AI to enrich, deduplicate, and prioritize alerts but require human decision-gates before closure or action.
source_title: Keynote: AI in CI/CD Without the Hype: Practical Patterns for Platform Engineers

claim: Secrets exposure risk: AI agents reading CI logs that contain environment variables must use self-hosted models or carefully scoped prompts stripping sensitive data.
actionable: Audit prompts sent to external APIs; strip secrets, credentials, and sensitive env vars before sending to third-party models.
source_title: Keynote: AI in CI/CD Without the Hype: Practical Patterns for Platform Engineers

claim: All AI service accounts need non-human identities with audit trails; without clear agent identity, you cannot enforce access controls or trace incidents.
actionable: Create distinct service accounts for each AI agent; audit all actions with real-time logging of prompts, tool invocations, and API calls.
source_title: Keynote: AI in CI/CD Without the Hype: Practical Patterns for Platform Engineers

claim: Earn trust through evidence, not enthusiasm: start read-only, measure outcomes in dev/staging, expand scope based on track record.
actionable: Pilot AI integrations in low-risk environments; measure impact; only expand permissions and scope after proving reliability.
source_title: Keynote: AI in CI/CD Without the Hype: Practical Patterns for Platform Engineers
